query: We're building a PWA Kit storefront and our SFRA site shares the same customer base. When a logged-in user clicks through to the SFRA pages they lose their session. How do I keep them authenticated across both storefronts?

Our checkout keeps getting 409 Conflict responses from the token endpoint when our backend service makes calls on behalf of shoppers. Two requests for the same customer hit within a couple seconds and the second one always fails. What's going on and how do we handle it?
I want to let shoppers on our site log in without a password — just send them an email with a code they type in. What's the flow for implementing that kind of passwordless auth on B2C Commerce?

Our access tokens are expiring mid-session and customers are getting logged out during checkout. I need to set up proper token refresh so the session stays alive, but I'm confused about when to use the refresh token vs requesting a new guest token. What's the right approach?

We have a microservice that needs to place orders on behalf of customers — trusted system on behalf of (TSOB) flow. The service has client credentials but I'm not sure how to exchange them for a token that represents a specific shopper. Walk me through the token exchange.

I'm getting a JWT back from the auth endpoint and I want to validate it server-side before trusting the claims. How do I verify the signature and what JWKS endpoint do I hit for the public keys? Also need to check the audience and issuer fields.
I need to set up a new API client in Account Manager so our CI pipeline can deploy code to our sandbox. What scopes should I configure?
How do I add products to a basket and start a checkout session from my composable storefront using the Shopper Baskets API?
I want to manage our SLAS admin settings — rotate the client secret for our tenant and update the redirect URIs. Where do I do that in Account Manager?
Our Shopper Baskets API call is returning a 401 and I think the issue is with the scope on the admin API client, not the shopper token. How do I check which scopes are assigned to a client ID?
